title: mentha glomerata var. palustris
intro: Mentha glomerata var. palustris, also known as marsh mint, is a variety of the Mentha glomerata plant, a species of mint native to New Zealand. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, and its essential oil has been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of marsh mint include relief from nausea, indigestion, and bloating, as well as its potential to ease respiratory issues such as congestion and coughs. Marsh mint can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a natural remedy in some cultures.
